incident_reconstruction.impact_direction: Frontal impact, likely at an angle given the damage to one side of the front bumper and headlight
incident_reconstruction.reconstruction: The vehicle likely swerved to avoid another vehicle and collided with a tree, causing a frontal impact that resulted in severe damage to the front bumper, moderate damage to the bonnet, and moderate damage to the left headlight.
reconstruction_bullets: The damage to the front bumper, bonnet, and left headlight is consistent with a frontal impact against a stationary object like a tree.
The severity of the damage suggests a significant force of impact, which aligns with the claimant's statement of taking a forceful turn to avoid another vehicle.
The damage pattern does not indicate a secondary impact or rollover, supporting the claimant's version of a single event.

storyboard_panels.title: Pre-Incident
storyboard_panels.description: The vehicle was being driven on a road when the driver encountered a vehicle stopped ahead.

storyboard_panels.title: Point of Impact
storyboard_panels.description: The driver swerved to avoid the stopped vehicle and hit a tree with the front of the vehicle.

storyboard_panels.title: Immediate Aftermath
storyboard_panels.description: The vehicle sustained significant front-end damage and was likely stopped by the impact.

storyboard_panels.title: Evidence Summary
storyboard_panels.description: The physical evidence supports the claimant's story, with damage consistent with a frontal impact against a tree.

incident_reconstruction.summary: Most Probable: Front impact | Confidence: 80% | Story Match: Yes

decision_reasons: Policy expired 65 days prior to incident, making claim ineligible (Policy Coverage Note)
Fraud score of 66 indicates potential workshop inflation and serial claimant behavior (Agent 2 findings)
Garage estimate exceeds AI-estimated repair cost by 100.6%, suggesting inflation (Agent 1 findings)
Claim amount exceeds total repair estimate, further indicating potential fraud (Claim Amount: ₹49,850, Total Repair Estimate: ₹36,100-₹63,600)
reasoning_trail: Step 1: Damage Assessment indicates severe damage but potential garage inflation
Step 2: Fraud Intelligence reveals medium fraud score with critical workshop inflation indicator
Step 3: Incident Reconstruction confirms front impact collision consistent with story
Step 4: Context & Policy verification shows policy expired 65 days prior to incident
Step 5: Historical Precedents suggest similar cases of workshop inflation and serial claimants (HIST-019, FS-004)
Step 6: Settlement Calculation is not applicable due to policy expiry and potential fraud
Step 7: Final Decision to reject claim based on policy expiry, potential fraud, and inflated garage estimate
